patches_reviewed.subject: Re: [PATCH V10 00/10] famfs: port into fuse
Re: [PATCH RFC v2 00/18] mm/virtio: skip redundant zeroing of host-zeroed reported pages
patches_reviewed.patch_summary: Proposal to skip redundant zeroing of host-zeroed reported pages in virtio mm code, with discussion around the feasibility and potential issues of leaking page information.

review_comments.author: Gregory Price
review_comments.summary: Identified lifecycle problems with leaking page information, suggested that plugging __GFP_ZERO correctly is the only feasible approach to avoid staleness issues.

review_comments.author: Michael S. Tsirkin
review_comments.summary: Agreed with Gregory's assessment and suggested a new version of the patch (v3) that addresses the concerns.

patches_reviewed.subject: Re: [RFC PATCH v6 2/5] mm: migrate: Add migrate_misplaced_folios_batch()
patches_reviewed.patch_summary: This patch is a response to an existing commit that added a function called migrate_misplaced_folios_batch(). The original author, Gregory Price, agrees that the approach taken in the original commit was reasonable but notes that it has some limitations. Specifically, he realized that folios may belong to different memory control groups (memcg), which means that bulk migration would need to process them into separate lists by memcg before migrating them.

review_comments.author: Bharata Rao
review_comments.summary:
Reviewer Bharata Rao noted that the patch description does not explicitly state that the caller of migrate_misplaced_folios_batch() ensures that the folios belong to the same memcg, and suggested adding a debug check to verify this assumption.

Reviewer noted that the function should immediately return if the source list is empty, especially considering the introduction of memcg awareness in this patch.

Reviewer noted that the original commit's assumption about folios belonging to the same memcg is likely incorrect, and a bulk migration may need to first process folios into lists by memcg before migrating them, requiring the commit to be redone.
review_comments.raw_body:
^ This is the expectation from the caller. pghot caller ensures this.
I could add a debug check in this routine to verify if that is indeed so.

---

Right. Earlier migrate_pages() may have handled empty source list, but
with memcg-awareness in this routine, it is better to return right away.

---

See above.

Regards,
Bharata.

review_comments.author: Gregory Price (author)
review_comments.summary: Author acknowledges that the patch likely needs to be redone due to an incorrect assumption about memcg ownership, and suggests revising the interface name for clarity.
review_comments.raw_body:
Wondering if the interface name should somehow reflect this as well, to
make it a bit clearer.

~Gregory

review_comments.author: Donet Tom
review_comments.summary:
Reviewer noted that the original commit's assumption about folios belonging to the same memcg is likely incorrect and may require a bulk migration to first process folios into lists by memcg before migrating them, suggesting the commit needs to be redone.

Reviewer Donet Tom agrees that making the interface clearer is a good idea, but notes that this commit likely needs to be redone due to an incorrect assumption about memcg ownership of folios.
review_comments.raw_body:
Hi Gregory

On 4/22/26 9:07 AM, Gregory Price wrote:

---

I agree\u2014this would make the interface clearer.

-Donet

review_comments.author: Bharata Rao
review_comments.summary: Reviewer agreed to make changes to the patch and acknowledged that it likely needs to be redone due to incorrect assumptions about memcg ownership of folios.

patches_reviewed.subject: Re: [PATCH V10 00/10] famfs: port into fuse
patches_reviewed.patch_summary: The patch aims to port the famfs filesystem into fuse, addressing performance issues from previous porting attempts.

review_comments.author: Gregory Price
review_comments.summary: Identified potential benefits of using a generic metadata blob in struct fuse_inode, but noted Christian Brauner's concern about dedicated bpf storage. Suggested exploring a custom hash table as an alternative.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Tue, Apr 21, 2026 at 11:59:15AM -0700, Joanne Koong wrote:
> On Tue, Apr 21, 2026 at 7:30\u202fAM Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net> wrote:
> 
> I'm not sure if this addresses Christian's concerns or not, but the
> blob would reside within struct fuse_inode not struct inode. I
> definitely agree with him that this should not touch or add any infra
> outside fuse.
> 
> I hadn't heard of bpf arenas until his comment. If the hashmap
> overhead is too high for famfs, having a custom in-arena hash table
> would be much faster I think, as it could be designed to require less
> pointer chasing and avoid other overhead in the bpf hashmap
> implementation, though now famfs would have to manage the data
> structure and complexity itself.
> 

I think if the fuse-inode blob is acceptable, that's highly preferable -
and it wouldn't necessarily need to stay specific to bpf.  If there was
some generic format that folks agreed on as a baseline, that could
obviously be worked into the interface.

~Gregory

review_comments.author: Joanne Koong
review_comments.summary: Addressed Christian's concerns by proposing the metadata blob reside within struct fuse_inode, rather than struct inode. Suggested a custom in-arena hash table as an alternative to bpf hashmap overhead.
review_comments.raw_body:
On Tue, Apr 21, 2026 at 7:30 AM Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net> wrote:
>
> On Mon, Apr 20, 2026 at 08:12:17PM -0700, Joanne Koong wrote:
> > On Sun, Apr 19, 2026 at 5:27 PM Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net> wrote:
> > >
> > >   struct fuse_dax_fmap_ops {
> > >       char name[FUSE_DAX_FMAP_OPS_NAME_LEN];   // 16 bytes
> > >       int (*dax_fmap_parse)(struct fuse_dax_fmap_parse_ctx *ctx);
> >
> > Just a note for later, if the bpf approach gets pursued further:
> > instead of making this a dax specific ops, I think this needs to be
> > integrated interface-wise with Darrick's fuse-iomap work since he does
> > the same thing. I think dax_fmap_parse() could be renamed to something
> > like iomap_setup(), where userspace can use this to do any sort of
> > generic setup, whether that's mapping related or dax related or not.
> > In my mind, the dax vs non dax distinction is handled by the fuse
> > iomap plumbing that chooses which iomap entry points to call,  but
> > beyond that,  the callbacks and struct ops themselves should be
> > generic enough to be shared between the two.
> >
>
> I think this is reasonable.  I'm not a FUSE wizard either, but I would
> presume the iomap_setup() process would just essentially be John's
> existing GET_FMAP / GET_DAXDEV code bundled.
>
> GET_DAXDEV happens lazily to save him the round-trips to userland if the
> DAXDEVs have already been seen previously.  I think your proposal does
> in fact save him further round trips, and it would probably solve the
> performance impact he saw from porting to FUSE.
>
> > > And otherwise, imap_begin() works exactly as Joanne proposed, but with
> > > in-kernel cached data instead of the bpfmap.
> > >
> > >   const struct dax_simple_meta *meta = (const struct dax_simple_meta *)
> > >                    bpf_fuse_dax_resolve_get_meta(ctx, 0, sizeof(*meta));
> >
> > another note for later, if the benchmarks prove promising and after
> > the LSF discussions we decide to go with this approach: imo we
> > could/should repurpose this into a generic
> > bpf_fuse_iomap_get_inode_meta() that returns a bounded pointer into
> > whatever opaque blob was cached on the inode during iomap_setup(),
> > where it'd be a generic kfunc serving both the dax and non-dax case
> > for any kind of mapping layout
> >
>
> Note that Christian Brauner just said he preferred not having dedicated
> bpf storage in struct inode [1].
>
> sans BPF, is there value in such a metadata blob existing?
>
> If there was a generic format, then I suppose the blob storage would not
> be BPF specific, it would just overload it (simple union).

I'm not sure if this addresses Christian's concerns or not, but the
blob would reside within struct fuse_inode not struct inode. I
definitely agree with him that this should not touch or add any infra
outside fuse.

I hadn't heard of bpf arenas until his comment. If the hashmap
overhead is too high for famfs, having a custom in-arena hash table
would be much faster I think, as it could be designed to require less
pointer chasing and avoid other overhead in the bpf hashmap
implementation, though now famfs would have to manage the data
structure and complexity itself.

Thanks,
Joanne

>
> [1] https://lore.kernel.org/linux-fsdevel/20260421-arsch-gelernt-e0b5bcd8a7ff@brauner/T/#m8fea90f5ed4a1b23bdc2563d978948b263b2030b
>
> ~Gregory

patches_reviewed.subject: Re: [PATCH RFC v2 00/18] mm/virtio: skip redundant zeroing of host-zeroed reported pages
patches_reviewed.patch_summary: This patch proposes to optimize the zeroing of host-zeroed pages in virtio by skipping redundant zeroing when the PG_zeroed bit is set. The approach involves adding a check in folio_zero_user() to skip zeroing but not cache flushing, and clearing the PG_zeroed bit. This change aims to reduce unnecessary work and improve performance.

review_comments.author: Michael Tsirkin
review_comments.summary:
Reviewer noted that implementing PG_zeroed check in folio_zero_user() would leak the page flag out of the buddy allocator, and suggested an alternative approach using ___put_folio(folio, gfp_t) to handle optional gfp flags on free

Reviewer noted that adding a gfp_t flag to indicate zeroing would abuse the existing gfp flags and suggested using optional gfp flags on free instead, which could be useful for certain callers.
review_comments.raw_body:
I do not see how - this would require leaking the page flag out of the
buddy allocator.

---

But we do not have a gfp_t flag meaning "this has been zeroed"
and when I proposed something similar in v1, David hated abusing
gfp flags for what is not an allocation property.

review_comments.author: Michael Tsirkin
review_comments.summary: Reviewer noted that replacing folio_zero_user() entirely is not feasible due to specific requirements of certain callers, such as hugetlbfs needing to zero its own pool, and suggested modifying the existing interface instead
review_comments.raw_body:
We can't completely replace it with GFP_ZERO though e.g. because hugetlbfs
has its own pool and needs to zero that.

review_comments.author: Gregory Price (author)
review_comments.summary: Author acknowledged Michael Tsirkin's feedback about propagating PG_zeroed bit and lifecycle problems, agreed that plugging __GFP_ZERO correctly is the feasible approach to avoid staleness issues.
review_comments.raw_body:
Right, but you're leaking that bit of information out one way or another
- whether it's a page-flag or something else (pghint_t) you have the
same lifecycle problems (when does it become invalidated? how long can
it be trusted for?).

I suppose at least with (pghint_t) the data (in theory) falls out of
scope and doesn't live with the page - but guaranteed it just ends up
polluting more and more interfaces.

I'm seeing why David's suggest to plumb __GFP_ZERO correctly makes
sense, it's really the only feasible approach here that doesn't generate
a staleness problem with whatever information you try to leak out.

~Gregory

patches_reviewed.subject: Re: [PATCH RFC v2 00/18] mm/virtio: skip redundant zeroing of host-zeroed reported pages

patches_acked.subject: Re: [PATCH] NFSD: Report whether fh_key was actually updated
